bugs.error_message: Minimap indicator handle briefly jumps upward each time new records arrive while tailing, producing a distracting jitter on a live log
bugs.root_cause: When line_count grows, the virtualizer's totalSize updates reactively before the deferred jumpToBottom RAF callback fires. minimapIndicator computes top = scrollTop / total * h with the old scrollTop against the new (larger) total, so the handle slides upward for a frame before the scroll catches up.
bugs.fix: In minimapIndicator, when props.tab.followTail.value is true, pin the handle to the bottom (top = h - height) instead of computing it from scrollTop. Independently improved follow UX: onViewportScroll now auto-enables followTail when the user lands within ROW_HEIGHT of the end via any path (scrollbar, minimap drag, jump-to-bottom button), matching the 'at the end = following' mental model.

bugs.error_message: Toggling the speed rail off and back on via Settings -> Slow requests -> Show speed rail leaves the rail blank after it remounts
bugs.root_cause: The rail is rendered with `v-if="speedRailVisible"`, which destroys the canvas DOM node when the toggle goes off and remounts a fresh blank one when it goes back on. The grid data stays in memory but nothing re-triggers paintSpeedRail on remount -- paintSpeedRail is only called from fetchSpeedGrid / fetchSpeedThresholds / themeObserver, none of which fire on a pure visibility flip.
bugs.fix: Added a `watch(speedRailVisible, ...)` in LogViewport that, when the computed flips to true, schedules a paintSpeedRail() in the next animation frame so Vue has finished re-mounting the canvas and the template ref is populated. Mirrors the pattern used for the minimap heatmap-blend repaint watcher.

bugs.error_message: TailState's head-hash rotation check flagged every append as a rotation when the file was shorter than HEAD_HASH_BYTES
bugs.root_cause: The hash was computed over min(HEAD_HASH_BYTES, current_size) on every poll. Growing the file from N to N+M bytes meant the next poll hashed N+M bytes -- a strictly longer prefix -- so the hash naturally differed and looked like a rotation.
bugs.fix: Pin head_prefix_len at construction (or after a reset_to/rotation) and rehash EXACTLY that prefix length on every subsequent poll. Growth keeps the anchored bytes unchanged so the hash is stable; only a rewrite flips it. Below-prefix-length shrinks are caught by the earlier size-shrink branch.

bugs.error_message: E0117 (orphan rule) when adding impl RecordScanner for Box<dyn RecordScanner + Send + Sync> in clog-app
bugs.root_cause: Both the trait and the Box type live outside clog-app, so the impl is orphan-forbidden. The motivation was to let the tail task hold a runtime-selected scanner across polls without caring whether it was a PatternLayout or a regex.
bugs.fix: Introduced a sized sum type (CompiledScanner { Pattern(CompiledPattern) | Regex(RegexScanner) }) that owns the impl in clog-app. Store ScannerKind (source strings) on OpenedFile and call ScannerKind::compile() each poll to materialise a CompiledScanner. Recompilation is cheap relative to disk I/O.

bugs.error_message: User reported: when detached (follow-tail off), incoming tail appends caused the viewport to visually shift/scroll, so users couldn't stably read a chosen section while tailing remained active.
bugs.root_cause: Two contributing causes. (1) On every Appended delta the UI was calling pages.value.delete(lastPage) followed by an implicit re-fetch via the virtualRows watcher. The delete blanked the cached page synchronously, so visible rows on that page rendered as empty placeholders until the next IPC round trip finished -- visually reading as a shift. (2) No defensive re-anchor of scrollTop across the line_count bump, so any internal virtualizer measurement that happened to nudge scrollTop would not be corrected.
bugs.fix: Replaced delete-then-refetch with an in-place force-refetch (fetchPage gained a `force` flag; cached entries stay rendered until the new payload arrives, then swap atomically via pages.value.set + new Map()). For the detached case, capture scrollTop before the count bump and re-assert it across two requestAnimationFrame ticks (one for the synchronous count change, one for any deferred virtualizer measurement). Follow-tail path still calls jumpToBottom() as before.

bugs.error_message: proptest 'prod_roundtrips' began failing 'must parse as header' on every input after extending BUILTIN_PATTERNS with new entries.
bugs.root_cause: The proptest indexed BUILTIN_PATTERNS by position (BUILTIN_PATTERNS[1].1) to look up the prod pattern. When new patterns were inserted, index 1 no longer pointed at prod, so the test compiled the wrong pattern and rejected every generated prod-shaped line. Same vulnerability existed in the unit tests for `parse_prod_header_no_logger` and `parse_log4j2_default_header`.
bugs.fix: Added a `builtin_pattern(name: &str) -> Option<&'static str>` lookup helper to clog-core::pattern (re-exported from lib.rs) and switched every test (unit + proptest) from BUILTIN_PATTERNS[i] indexing to builtin_pattern("name").unwrap(). The position-stable lookup makes future reordering safe.

bugs.error_message: Uncaught ReferenceError: Cannot access 'effectiveCount' before initialization (at ComputedRefImpl.fn in App.vue) -- app opens blank
bugs.root_cause: In <script setup>, useVirtualizer(computed(() => ({ count: effectiveCount.value, ... }))) was called BEFORE effectiveCount itself was declared. @tanstack/vue-virtual reads the supplied computed synchronously during its setup to initialise internal state, which evaluates the getter immediately. Const declarations are in the temporal dead zone before their initialiser runs, so the getter threw on first access and Vue mount failed before the template ever rendered.
bugs.fix: Move filter-related computeds (filteredLineIndices, effectiveCount, actualLineIndex) ABOVE the useVirtualizer call so they are initialised by the time the virtualizer reads them. lineRowVirtual stays after lineRow since it is only invoked from the template (which runs post-mount). Lesson: in script setup, any computed/function whose value is read synchronously during another setup call must be declared before that call.

bugs.error_message: OpenWolf post-write.js hook silently overwrites .wolf/anatomy.md with a one-file stub on every Edit/Write to a non-.wolf/ file. The destruction looked like a cron firing but cron-state.json showed execution_log: [] -- it was the write hook.
bugs.root_cause: shared.js::parseAnatomy regex demands the strict OpenWolf format: backtick-wrapped filename, em-dash separator ( -- ), and a trailing (~N tok) token suffix. The project's anatomy.md was hand-maintained in plain ASCII per CLAUDE.md's global rule (ASCII hyphen, no token counts), so parseAnatomy returned an empty sections Map. The hook then added only the just-edited file to the empty map and serializeAnatomy wrote a stub with `Files: 1 tracked`. A bare `catch {}` around the whole anatomy-update block hid the parse mismatch. Every non-.wolf/ Edit/Write triggered the same destruction silently.
bugs.fix: Added an 'ASCII-only rule exception' subsection to CLAUDE.md carving .wolf/-managed files out of the global ASCII rule -- they must use the em-dash + (~N tok) format the hook's parser expects. Rebuilt anatomy.md via `openwolf scan` (88 files indexed) so the on-disk format now matches the hook's parser; subsequent edits round-trip cleanly. Future hand-edits to .wolf/anatomy.md must match the canonical format or the hook will drop them on the next write.

bugs.error_message: Dragging from inside a modal input out onto the backdrop and releasing (a normal text-selection gesture) closes the modal.
bugs.root_cause: BaseModal used @click.self on the backdrop. The synthesised click event fires when both mousedown and mouseup happen on the same element OR on certain drag/release sequences; in particular, the click target resolves to the lowest common ancestor of mousedown and mouseup, which is the backdrop when the press starts in a child input and releases on the backdrop. @click.self then matched and closed.
bugs.fix: Replaced backdrop @click.self with paired @mousedown / @mouseup handlers that close only when BOTH events targeted the backdrop element itself. A drag that begins inside any modal child no longer counts as a backdrop click.
